ucs.title: [LLM] Leafminer (Raspite) MailSniper-style password spray against Exchange/OWA followed by successful logon
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Leafminer initial-access chain documented by Symantec: enumerate domain users via Outlook Web App, then conduct low-and-slow password spraying against Exchange/OWA (often via legacy/basic-auth endpoints) and pivot the successful credential into mail collection. Catches the exact tradecraft Symantec attributed to G0077 in 2017-2018 Middle East intrusions where MailSniper was the primary external auth-abuse vector.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count as failures values(Authentication.user) as failed_users min(_time) as first_fail max(_time) as last_fail values(Authentication.app) as apps from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="failure" AND (Authentication.app="*Exchange*" OR Authentication.app="*Outlook*" OR Authentication.app="*OWA*" OR Authentication.app="*ActiveSync*" OR Authentication.app="*IMAP*" OR Authentication.app="*POP*") by Authentication.src | rename Authentication.* as * | eval failed_user_count=mvcount(failed_users) | where failed_user_count >= 10 AND failures >= failed_user_count | join type=inner src [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as success_time values(Authentication.user) as compromised_user values(Authentication.app) as success_app from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="success" AND (Authentication.app="*Exchange*" OR Authentication.app="*Outlook*" OR Authentication.app="*OWA*") by Authentication.src | rename Authentication.* as * ] | where success_time >= first_fail AND success_time <= (last_fail + 21600) | table first_fail last_fail src apps failed_user_count failures failed_users success_time compromised_user success_app
ucs.kql:
// Leafminer (Raspite) — OWA/Exchange password spray → successful logon
// Threshold: 10 unique failed accounts/IP within 24h is the empirical floor
// for Leafminer's slow-spray cadence (Symantec, July 2018)
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let SprayUserThreshold = 10;
let PwdSprayCodes = dynamic([50053, 50126, 50055, 50056, 50057, 50064]); // bad pwd / locked / disabled
let SprayingIPs = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where (ResourceDisplayName has_any ("Exchange", "Outlook")
          or AppDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange Online", "Outlook Web App", "Outlook"))
    | where ErrorCode in (PwdSprayCodes)
    // Leafminer favours legacy/non-MFA endpoints — keep but don't require
    | where ClientAppUsed in~ ("Other clients","IMAP","POP","Exchange ActiveSync","Authenticated SMTP","Browser")
    | summarize FailedUsers = dcount(AccountUpn),
                Failures   = count(),
                AffectedAccounts = make_set(AccountUpn, 50),
                FirstFail  = min(Timestamp),
                LastFail   = max(Timestamp)
                by IPAddress, Country
    | where FailedUsers >= SprayUserThreshold;
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackHours)
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where (ResourceDisplayName has_any ("Exchange","Outlook")
      or AppDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange Online","Outlook Web App","Outlook"))
| join kind=inner SprayingIPs on IPAddress
| where Timestamp between (FirstFail .. LastFail + 6h)   // success within 6h of last spray hit
| project SuccessTime = Timestamp, CompromisedAccount = AccountUpn,
          IPAddress, Country, FailedUsers, Failures,
          FirstFail, LastFail, AffectedAccounts,
          SuccessApp = AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent
| order by SuccessTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Leafminer (Raspite) — OWA/Exchange password spray → successful logon
let LookbackHours = 24h;
let SprayUserThreshold = 10;
let PwdSprayCodes = dynamic([50053, 50126, 50055, 50056, 50057, 50064]);
let SprayingIPs = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
    | where (ResourceDisplayName has_any ("Exchange","Outlook")
          or AppDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange Online","Outlook Web App","Outlook"))
    | where ResultType in (PwdSprayCodes)
    | where ClientAppUsed in~ ("Other clients","IMAP","POP","Exchange ActiveSync","Authenticated SMTP","Browser")
    | summarize FailedUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
                Failures   = count(),
                AffectedAccounts = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 50),
                FirstFail  = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastFail   = max(TimeGenerated)
                by IPAddress
    | where FailedUsers >= SprayUserThreshold;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackHours)
| where ResultType == 0
| where (ResourceDisplayName has_any ("Exchange","Outlook")
      or AppDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange Online","Outlook Web App","Outlook"))
| join kind=inner SprayingIPs on IPAddress
| where TimeGenerated between (FirstFail .. LastFail + 6h)
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
| project SuccessTime = TimeGenerated, CompromisedAccount = UserPrincipalName,
          IPAddress, Country, FailedUsers, Failures, FirstFail, LastFail,
          AffectedAccounts, SuccessApp = AppDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent
| order by SuccessTime desc
ucs.rationale: Leafminer's external playbook (per Symantec's 2018 report) is unusually distinctive: OWA-based user enumeration via MailSniper, then patient password spraying that sprays ~10–30 accounts per source IP at a rate well below per-account lockout thresholds. The high-fidelity differentiator versus generic spray is the SAME source IP that just hit a wide failed-user fan-out then succeeds against an Exch

ucs.title: [LLM] Leafminer (Raspite) LaZagne / FireMalv credential harvesting on host
ucs.description: Hunts execution of the public LaZagne credential dumper and Leafminer's custom 'FireMalv' Firefox-credential-stealer — the two tools Symantec specifically attributed to G0077 for post-exploitation credential collection from browsers, Windows Vault, mail clients and Wi-Fi profiles. Pairs T1555.003 (browser stores), T1552.001 (creds in files) and T1003.005 (cached domain creds) into one process-creation signal.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as hashes from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("lazagne.exe","laZagne.exe","firemalv.exe") OR Processes.process="*lazagne*" OR Processes.process="*LaZagne*" OR Processes.process="*FireMalv*" OR (Processes.process_name IN ("python.exe","pythonw.exe") AND Processes.process="*all*" AND (Processes.process="*browsers*" OR Processes.process="*windows*" OR Processes.process="*mails*" OR Processes.process="*wifi*" OR Processes.process="*chats*"))) AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name | rename Processes.* as * | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Leafminer (Raspite) — LaZagne / FireMalv credential dumper execution
// Tool attribution: Symantec 2018 names LaZagne and the custom FireMalv
// Firefox-cred-stealer as Leafminer's two go-to credential-collection tools.
let LookbackDays = 14d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where
    // 1. Direct binary / cmdline tool names
    FileName has_any ("lazagne","firemalv")
    or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lazagne","LaZagne","FireMalv")
    or InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("lazagne","firemalv")
    // 2. PE product-info leak (LaZagne PyInstaller builds keep "LaZagne")
    or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has "LaZagne"
    or ProcessVersionInfoFileDescription has "LaZagne"
    or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoProductName has "LaZagne"
    // 3. Python LaZagne with the canonical "all" / category subcommands
    or ( FileName in~ ("python.exe","pythonw.exe")
         and ProcessCommandLine has "all"
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("browsers","windows","mails","wifi","chats","sysadmin"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ToolImage = FolderPath, ToolFile = FileName, SHA256,
          ProductName = ProcessVersionInfoProductName,
          FileDesc    = ProcessVersionInfoFileDescription,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentFile  = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Leafminer (Raspite) — LaZagne / FireMalv credential dumper execution
// Requires GPO 'Include command line in process creation events' for 4688
let LookbackDays = 14d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountType == "User"
| where
    NewProcessName has_any ("lazagne","firemalv")
    or CommandLine has_any ("lazagne","LaZagne","FireMalv")
    or ParentProcessName has_any ("lazagne","firemalv")
    or ( NewProcessName endswith "\\python.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\pythonw.exe" )
        and CommandLine has "all"
        and CommandLine has_any ("browsers","windows","mails","wifi","chats","sysadmin")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, LogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Leafminer (Raspite) LaZagne / FireMalv Credential Harvester Execution
id: 9f4d5b3a-6e2c-4ad7-9f10-1aa5b2c0d4e7
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of the public LaZagne credential dumper and Leafminer's custom FireMalv Firefox-credential-stealer, both attributed to the Iranian threat group Leafminer (Raspite, MITRE G0077) by Symantec for credential harvesting from browsers, Windows Vault, mail clients and Wi-Fi profiles.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0077/
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/leafminer-espionage-middle-east
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_name:
    Image|contains:
      - '\lazagne'
      - '\firemalv'
  selection_cmdline:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'lazagne'
      - 'LaZagne'
      - 'FireMalv'
  selection_pyinstaller:
    Product|contains: 'LaZagne'
  selection_python:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\python.exe'
      - '\pythonw.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'browsers all'
      - 'windows all'
      - 'mails all'
      - 'wifi all'
      - '-m laZagne'
      - '-m lazagne'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_name or selection_cmdline or selection_pyinstaller or selection_python) and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Authorised red-team / penetration-test exercises using LaZagne for credential audit
  - IT-admin password-recovery scripts using the same tool name
level: high
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.005
  - attack.t1552.001
  - attack.t1555
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.g0077
ucs.rationale: Leafminer is one of a handful of named groups where Symantec specifically called out the post-exploitation credential-collection toolset by name: LaZagne (publicly available) and FireMalv (custom, Firefox-targeted). Hunting on those tool names plus the canonical LaZagne subcommand surface (`all`, `browsers`, `mails`, `wifi`) is far more actor-specific than a generic 'creds-from-browsers' rule, whi
